title: Per-Origin Politeness, Token Timing, Retry Budgets, and Fairness · Go Retrieval
summary: A delayed operations review reconstructs per-origin politeness, token timing, retry budgets, and fairness from revision, authorization, seed, URL, robots decision, request, response bytes, tree identity, record provenance, frontier transition, goroutine owner, report row, failure, cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for monotonic per-origin eligibility scheduler after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-…
objectives: Track per-origin active requests, last start, next eligibility, request and byte budgets, robots state, and owner override.
Enforce minimum spacing between request starts with monotonic time and avoid holding shared locks while sleeping.
Classify transport and HTTP failures by method safety, response evidence, attempt, elapsed, and crawl budgets.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review Go team handles case 5327 during a malformed HTML and parser differential clinic. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove per-origin state, start spacing, retry classes, Retry-After, bounded jitter, and fairness; failed, canceled, stale, over-budget, and revoked work preserves prior admitted state; then inject one session, secret, SSRF, output, path, log, privacy, or retention fault and contain every derivative. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Select among eligible origins with starvation bounds, stable priorities, owner commitments, and visible queue-age evidence.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: One FIFO channel is fair across origins.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Select among eligible origins with starvation bounds, stable priorities, owner commitments, and visible queue-age evidence. Artifact under review: monotonic per-origin eligibility scheduler.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Origin Fairness, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Origin Fairness record says monotonic per-origin eligibility scheduler looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Origin Fairness.

For separate evidence from confidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof. This does not satisfy Origin Fairness for monotonic per-origin eligibility scheduler.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Origin Fairness
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Origin Fairness: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Go team handles case 3708 during a mixed-origin fairness and race rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove per-origin state, start spacing, retry classes, Retry-After, bounded jitter, and fairness; browser Go remains deterministic and free of native network, parser, filesystem, process, and host effects; then change one fuzz, race, mutation, capacity, binary, deployment, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Select among eligible origins with starvation bounds, stable priorities, owner commitments, and visible queue-age evidence.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Origin Fairness.
steps.why: Retrieving Origin Fairness through the concrete monotonic per-origin eligibility scheduler case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Authorized site auditing and Go Record what required effort · Review Origin Fairness, keep Origin Fairness connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Select among eligible origins with starvation bounds, stable priorities, owner commitments, and visible queue-age evidence.
